questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘SOURAV’ is written as ‘90’, ‘ROHAN’ is written as ‘51’, how will ‘KAUSHAL’ be written in that language?
questions.explanation: As per the alphabetical order, S=19, O=15, U=21, R=18, A=1, V=22 So, 19+15+21+18+1+22=96 and 96 -No. of Alphabets= 96 -6=90 Also, in ROHAN, 18+15+8+1+14=56 and 56-No. of Alphabets= 56-5=51 Therefore, in KAUSHAL→ 11+1+21+19+8+1+12=73 and 73-7=66

questions.question: In 1856, Governor-General ______ decided that Bahadur Shah Zafar would be the last Mughal king and after his death none of his descendants would be recognised as kings, they would just be called princes? > 1856 में, गवर्नर-जनरल ______ ने निर्णय लिया कि बहादुर शाह ज़फर अंतिम मुग़ल सम्राट होंगे और उनकी मृत्यु के बाद उनके किसी भी वंशज को सम्राट के रूप में मान्यता नहीं दी जाएगी, उन्हें केवल राजकुमार कहा जाएगा?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar would be the last Mughal king and after his death none of his descendants would be recognised as kings, they would just be called princes this whole decision was taken by then G.G. Canning in 1856. Canning became the first Viceroy of India in 1857. > बहादुर शाह ज़फर अंतिम मुग़ल सम्राट होंगे और उनकी मृत्यु के बाद उनके किसी भी वंशज को सम्राट के रूप में मान्यता नहीं दी जाएगी, उन्हें केवल राजकुमार कहा जाएगा। यह पूरा निर्णय तब के गवर्नर जनरल कैनिंग द्वारा 1856 में लिया गया था। कैनिंग 1857 में भारत के पहले वायसराय बने।

questions.question: Who was considered as the last powerful Mughal ruler of India? > भारत के अंतिम शक्तिशाली मुग़ल शासक के रूप में किसे माना गया?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was the sixth Mughal Emperor of India. Also known as ‘Alamgir’, he ruled from 1658 to 1707. Under his rule, the Mughal Empire reached its zenith and became the single largest empire in India. He re-introduced Jaziya. Futuhat-i-Alamgiri was written by Ishwardas Nagar, a Mughal officer in Jodhpur. The book is a chronicle of the reign of Mughal emperor Aurangzeb. > औरंगजेब भारत के छठे मुग़ल सम्राट थे। उन्हें 'आलमगीर' के नाम से भी जाना जाता है, उन्होंने 1658 से 1707 तक शासन किया। उनके शासन के तहत, मुग़ल साम्राज्य अपने चरम पर पहुंच गया और भारत का सबसे बड़ा साम्राज्य बन गया। उन्होंने जज़िया को फिर से लागू किया। फ़ुतूहट ए आलंगीरी को ईश्वरदास नागर द्वारा लिखा,जो जोधपुर में एक मुग़ल अधिकारी थे। यह पुस्तक मुग़ल सम्राट औरंगजेब के शासनकाल का एक इतिहास है।

questions.question: In which year did Tipu Sultan die while defending his capital, Seringapatam? > टीपू सुल्तान की मृत्यु किस वर्ष अपनी राजधानी सेरिंगपट्टनम की रक्षा करते समय हुई थी?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan commonly referred to as Sher-e-Mysore or "Tiger of Mysore” was a ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore based in South India. He died while defending his capital Srirangapatam in the fourth Anglo-Mysore battle which was fought between the Kingdom of Mysore and the British East India Company led by Arthur Wellesley. > टीपू सुल्तान जिन्हे मैसूर का शेर या मैसूर का बाघ कहते है, दक्षिण भारत मे स्थित मैसूर राज्य के शासक थे। चौथे एंग्लो-मैसूर युद्ध में अपनी राजधानी श्रीरंगपट्टनम की रक्षा करते हुए उनकी मृत्यु हो गई। यह युद्ध मैसूर साम्राज्य और आर्थर वेलेस्ली के नेतृत्व वाली ब्रिटिश ईस्ट इंडिया कंपनी के बीच लड़ा गया था।

questions.question: Which of the following is not related to Kirana Gharana? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा किराना घराना से संबंधित नहीं है?
questions.explanation: Malini Rajukar is related to Gwalior Gharana of Khyal Gayaki. Excluding her all other options are related to Kirana Gharana, in which the swara is used to create on emotional mood by means of elongation. Founder of Kirana Ghrana was Abdul Karim Khan and Abdul Wahid Khan. Other related person to Kirana Gharana are Bhimsen Joshi Begum Akhtar, etc. > मालिनी राजुकर ख्याल गायकी के ग्वालियर घराने से संबंधित हैं। उन्हें छोड़कर अन्य सभी विकल्प किराना घराने से संबंधित हैं, जिसमें स्वर का उपयोग भावनात्मक मूड बनाने के लिए किया जाता है। किराना घराने के संस्थापक अब्दुल करीम खान और अब्दुल वाहिद खान थे। किराना घराने से संबंधित अन्य व्यक्ति भीमसेन जोशी, बेगम अख्तर आदि हैं।

questions.question: Which Indian classical musician has an auditorium in Tehran, Iran, named in his honor? > किस भारतीय शास्त्रीय संगीतकार के सम्मान में तेहरान, ईरान में एक ऑडिटोरियम का नाम रखा गया है?
questions.explanation: Indian musician Ustad Bismillah Khan is recognised for extraordinary expertise in the instrument shehnai. In Teheran, Iran, there is an auditorium with his name on it. In 2001, he received the Bharat Ratna along with Lata Mangeshkar. >भारतीय संगीतकार उस्ताद बिस्मिल्लाह खान को शहनाई वाद्य में असाधारण कौशल के लिए पहचाना जाता है। तेहरान, ईरान में उनके नाम पर एक ऑडिटोरियम है। 2001 में, उन्हें लता मंगेशकर के साथ भारत रत्न मिला।

questions.question: Who among the followings was popularly known as the lighthouse of Rebellion in the Awadh region and led the 1857 uprising at Faizabad? > नीचे दिए गए में से कौन अवध क्षेत्र में विद्रोह का लाइटहाउस के रूप में लोकप्रिय था और उसने फैज़ाबाद में 1857 के विद्रोह का नेतृत्व किया?
questions.explanation: Ahmadullah shah, popularly known as Maulavi of Faizabad, was one of the great leaders of the great Indian revolt of 1857. He was famously known as the lighthouse of rebellion in the Awadh region. > अहमदुल्ला शाह, जिन्हें फैज़ाबाद के मौलवी के रूप में जाना जाता था, 1857 के महान भारतीय विद्रोह के महान नेताओं में से एक थे। उन्हें अवध क्षेत्र में विद्रोह का लाइटहाउस के रूप में प्रसिद्धि मिली।

questions.question: Who among the following was the first female from India to receive the Nobel Peace prize? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन भारत की पहली महिला थी जिसे नोबेल शांति पुरस्कार मिला?
questions.explanation: Mother Teresa was a Roman Catholic nun and missionary who dedicated her life to helping the poor and vulnerable. She was born in what is now Macedonia, and lived and worked in India [came to India in 1929] for nearly seven decades. Mother Teresa was the first woman who was the close Indian ties to get the 1979 Nobel Peace Prize. She lost her life on 5 September 1997. > मदर टेरेसा एक रोमन कैथोलिक नन और मिशनरी थीं जिन्होंने गरीबों और कमजोरों की मदद करने के लिए अपना जीवन समर्पित किया। उनका जन्म वर्तमान मैसेडोनिया में हुआ था, और उन्होंने लगभग सात दशकों तक भारत में रहकर काम किया [1929 में भारत आईं}। मदर टेरेसा पहली महिला थीं जिनका भारत से निकट संबंध था और जिन्होंने 1979 का नोबेल शांति पुरस्कार प्राप्त किया।

questions.question: Who has been appointed Secretary of the Department of Space and Chairman of ISRO? > किसे अंतरिक्ष विभाग का सचिव और इसरो का अध्यक्ष नियुक्त किया गया है?
questions.explanation: Dr. V. Narayanan, a scientist experienced in rocket and spacecraft propulsion, has been appointed Secretary of the Department of Space and Chairman of ISRO, will be taking his charge from January 14, 2025. He will succeed S. Somanath. Dr Vikram Ambalal Sarabhai was one of the greatest scientists of India widely regarded as the father of the Indian space programme. > रॉकेट और अंतरिक्ष यान प्रोपल्शन में अनुभवी वैज्ञानिक डॉ. वी. नारायणन को अंतरिक्ष विभाग का सचिव और इसरो के अध्यक्ष नियुक्त किया गया है, जो 14 जनवरी, 2025 से अपना कार्यभार संभालेंगे। वह एस. सोमनाथ का स्थान लेंगे। डॉ. विक्रम अम्बालाल साराभाई भारत के सबसे महान वैज्ञानिकों में से एक थे, जिन्हें भारतीय अंतरिक्ष कार्यक्रम के पिता के रूप में व्यापक रूप से माना जाता है।

questions.question: Where was the head office of the Indian National Army [set up by Subhash Chandra Bose] located? > भारतीय राष्ट्रीय सेना का मुख्यालय कहा स्थित है जिसे सुभाष चंद्र बोस ने स्थापित किया था?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Army [INA] was formed by Mohan Singh and Iwaichi Fujiwara in 1942. However, Mohan Singh was arrested and the First INA was disbanded after he refused to align with the Japanese. Subhash Chandra Bose later reorganized the INA and recruited many Indian civilians from Malaya and Singapore. Subhash Chandra Bose set up two Indian National Army Headquarters, at Rangoon and Singapore. > भारतीय राष्ट्रीय सेना का गठन मोहन सिंह और इवाइची फुजीवारा ने 1942 में किया था। हालांकि, मोहन सिंह को गिरफ्तार कर लिया गया और पहले INA को भंग कर दिया गया जब उन्होंने जापानियों के साथ सहयोग करने से इनकार कर दिया। सुभाष चंद्र बोस ने बाद में INA का पुनर्गठन किया और मलय और सिंगापुर से कई भारतीय नागरिकों की भर्ती की। सुभाष चंद्र बोस ने रंगून और सिंगापुर में दो भारतीय राष्ट्रीय सेना मुख्यालय स्थापित किए।

questions.question: Which of the following is a snow-fed river? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी एक नदी है, जो हिमनद से जल पाती है?
questions.explanation: Yamuna is a snow-fed river because it originates from Yamunotri glacier near Bandarpoonch peak of greater Himalaya. Yamuna is the longest tributary of river Ganga [Longest River in India]. > यमुना एक हिमनद से जल पाने बाली नदी है क्योंकि यह ग्रेटर हिमालय के बंदरपूनच पीक के पास यमुनोत्री ग्लेशियर से निकलती है। यमुना गंगा नदी की सबसे लंबी सहायक नदी है।

questions.question: Named the largest desert which is located in the southern hemisphere? > दक्षिणी गोलार्ध में स्थित सबसे बड़े रेगिस्तान का नाम क्या है?
questions.explanation: The Antarctic Desert is the largest desert in the world and is located in the southern hemisphere. It covers 14,200,000 square kilometres. Other prominent desert in the Southern Hemisphere is the Kalahari Desert in Southern Africa, the Namib Desert [coastal Namibia], the Atacama Desert [Chile], and the Great Sandy Desert [Australia]. > अंटार्कटिक रेगिस्तान दुनिया का सबसे बड़ा रेगिस्तान है और यह दक्षिणी गोलार्ध में स्थित है। यह 14,200,000 वर्ग किलोमीटर तक फैला हुआ है। दक्षिणी गोलार्ध में अन्य प्रमुख रेगिस्तान कालाहारी रेगिस्तान [दक्षिण अफ्रीका], नामिब रेगिस्तान [तटीय नामीबिया], अटाकामा रेगिस्तान [चिली], दी ग्रेट सैन्डी रेगिस्तान [ऑस्ट्रेलिया]

questions.question: The solar system’s mass located in? > सौर मंडल का द्रव्यमान कहाँ स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The majority of the solar system's mass is located in the Sun. It contains over 99.8% of the total mass within the solar system. The Sun's immense gravitational pull is what keeps all the planets and other celestial bodies in orbit around it. > सौर मंडल का अधिकांश द्रव्यमान सूर्य में स्थित है। इसमें सौर मंडल के कुल द्रव्यमान का 99.8% से अधिक शामिल है। सूर्य का विशाल गुरुत्वाकर्षण सभी ग्रहों और अन्य आकाशीय पिंडों को इसके चारों ओर कक्षा में बनाए रखता है।

questions.question: Uttarakhand state hosted the 38th National Games in 2024. How many medals did Uttarakhand win in the 38th National Games 2024? > उत्तराखंड राज्य ने 2024 में 38वें राष्ट्रीय खेलों की मेज़बानी की। उत्तराखंड ने 38वें राष्ट्रीय खेलों 2024 में कितने पदक जीते?
questions.explanation: 38th National Games concluded at the International Sports Stadium, Haldwani, in Uttarakhand. Top Scorer were Services, Maharashtra, and Haryana in the total tally. Yoga and Mallakhamb are introduced for the first time. Uttarakhand won a record 103 medals, including 24 golds, inspiring local athletes. Services won the 38th National Games with 121 medals: 68 gold, 26 silver, and 27 bronze. > 38वें राष्ट्रीय खेलों का समापन उत्तराखंड के हल्द्वानी में अंतरराष्ट्रीय खेल स्टेडियम में हुआ। कुल तालिका में शीर्ष स्कोरर Services, महाराष्ट्र और हरियाणा थे। योग और मल्लखंभ पहली बार पेश किए गए। उत्तराखंड ने 24 स्वर्ण सहित 103 पदक जीते, जिससे स्थानीय एथलीटों को प्रेरणा मिली। Services ने 121 पदकों के साथ 38वें राष्ट्रीय खेलों में जीत हासिल की जिसमे 68 स्वर्ण, 26 रजत और 27 कांस्य पदक रहे।

questions.question: A Central Sector Scheme designed to offer comprehensive support to artisans and craftspeople working with traditional tools? > एक केंद्रीय क्षेत्र योजना जो पारंपरिक उपकरणों के साथ काम करने वाले कारीगरों और शिल्पकारों को व्यापक समर्थन प्रदान करने के लिए डिज़ाइन की गई है?
questions.explanation: Launched on September 17, 2023, by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the PM Vishwakarma Yojana is a Central Sector Scheme designed to offer comprehensive support to artisans and craftspeople working with traditional tools. > 17 सितंबर, 2023 को प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी द्वारा लॉन्च की गई पीएम विश्वकर्मा योजना एक केंद्रीय क्षेत्र की योजना है जो पारंपरिक उपकरणों के साथ काम करने वाले कारीगरों और शिल्पकारों को व्यापक समर्थन प्रदान करने के लिए बनाई गई है।

questions.question: Which of the following article of Indian constitution related to marshal law? > भारतीय संविधान का कौन सा अनुच्छेद मार्शल लॉ से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Article 34 of Indian constitution related to marshal law. Article 33 of Indian constitution related to power of parliament to restrict or abrogate rights of armed forces. Article 355 of Indian constitution related to duty of the Union to protect State against external aggression and internal disturbance. Article 35 of Indian constitution related to power of parliament to make law that enforce fundamental rights. > भारतीय संविधान का अनुच्छेद 34 मार्शल लॉ से संबंधित है। भारतीय संविधान का अनुच्छेद 33 सशस्त्र बलों के अधिकारों को सीमित या समाप्त करने के लिए संसद की शक्ति से संबंधित है। भारतीय संविधान का अनुच्छेद 355 संघ की राज्य को बाहरी आक्रमण और आंतरिक अशांति से सुरक्षा करने की जिम्मेदारी से संबंधित है। भारतीय संविधान का अनुच्छेद 35 संसद को मौलिक अधिकारों को लागू करने के लिए कानून बनाने की शक्ति से संबंधित है।

questions.question: Match the following: Parts of Constitution Related to A) Part IXA 1) Schedule and tribal areas B) Part XIV 2) Panchayats C) Part X 3) Municipalities D) Part IX 4) UPSC
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution is divided into 25 parts and 12 schedules. It had 395 articles in 22 parts and 8 schedules at the time of its commencement.

questions.explanation: The 09 coastal states of India from longest to shortest coastline are: Gujarat: Has the longest coastline in India at over 1,600 km, Andhra Pradesh [973.7 km], Tamil Nadu: [906.9 km], Maharashtra: [652.6 km], Kerala: [569.7 km], Odisha: [476.4 km], Karnataka: [280 km], West Bengal: [157.5 km], Goa: [101 km] > भारत के 09 तटीय राज्यों की सबसे लंबी से सबसे छोटी तटरेखा इस प्रकार है: गुजरात 1,600 किमी से अधिक है, आंध्र प्रदेश - 973.7 किमी, तमिलनाडु-906.9 किमी, महाराष्ट्र -652.6 किमी, केरल- 569.7 किमी, ओडिशा-476.4 किमी, कर्नाटक-280 किमी, पश्चिम बंगाल-157.5 किमी, गोवा-101 किमी

questions.question: Complex food substances break down into simpler ones with the help of? > जटिल खाद्य पदार्थ सरल पदार्थों में टूट जाते हैं किसकी मदद से?
questions.explanation: Enzymes are proteins that break down food into nutrients that your body can absorb. They speed up chemical reactions that turn food into energy. Amylase: Breaks down carbohydrates and starches, Lipase: Breaks down fats, Protease: Works on proteins, Lactase: Breaks down lactose, Sucrase: Breaks down sucrose. > एंजाइम एक प्रकार के प्रोटीन होते हैं जो खाद्य पदार्थों को पोषक तत्वों में तोड़ते हैं जिन्हें आपका शरीर अवशोषित कर सकता है। वे रासायनिक प्रतिक्रियाओं को तेज करते हैं जो खाद्य पदार्थों को ऊर्जा में बदलते है। एनजाइम्स:- Amylase: ये carbohydrates और स्टार्च को तोड़ते है, Lipase: ये वसा को तोड़ते है, Protease: ये प्रोटीन्स को तोड़ते है, Lactase: ये लैक्टोस को तोड़ते है, Sucrase: ये सूक्रोस को तोड़ते है।

questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा गलत है?
options: GDPFC = GDPₘₚ – Indirect Tax + Subsidy>GDPFC = GDPMP – अप्रत्यक्ष कर + सब्सिडी
GDPFC = GDPₘₚ – Tax + Subsidy >GDPFC = GDPMP – कर + सब्सिडी
GDPFC = GDPₘₚ – Net indirect Tax >GDPFC = GDPMP – निवल अप्रत्यक्ष कर
GDPFC = GDPₘₚ – Subsidy + Tax>GDPFC = GDPMP – सब्सिडी + कर
questions.explanation: GDPF C stands for Gross Domestic Product at Factor Cost. GDP is calculated at the market price [GDPₘₚ], which signifies that the value of production is calculated by multiplying the price that buyers pay and not the price which producing units actually receive. GDPFC = GDPₘₚ − Net indirect taxes [– Indirect Tax + Subsidy]. > GDPFC का अर्थ है कारक लागत पर सकल घरेलू उत्पाद। GDP को बाजार मूल्य [GDPₘₚ] पर गणना की जाती है, जिसका अर्थ है कि उत्पादन का मूल्य उन कीमतों को गुणा करके गणना की जाती है जो खरीदार चुकाते हैं, न कि उन कीमतों से जो उत्पादन इकाइयाँ वास्तव में प्राप्त करती हैं। जीडीपीएफसी = जीडीपीएमपी − शुद्ध अप्रत्यक्ष कर [– अप्रत्यक्ष कर + सब्सिडी]।

questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest acid known to humans? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा मानव जाति के लिए ज्ञात सबसे मजबूत अम्ल है?
questions.explanation: Fluoroantimonic acid is a mixture of hydrogen fluoride and antimony penta­fluoride, containing various cations and anions. Fluoroantimonic acid has a pH of about -31.3. It's a superacid that's more than 10¹⁶ times stronger than pure sulfuric acid. The chemical formula for fluoroantimonic acid is HSbF₆. > फ्लोरोएंटिमोनिक एसिड हाइड्रोजन फ्लोराइड और एंटीमनी पेंटाफ्लोराइड का मिश्रण है, जिसमें विभिन्न कैटायन और एनायन होते हैं। फ्लोरोएंटिमोनिक एसिड का pH लगभग -31.3 है। यह एक सुपरएसिड है जो शुद्ध सल्फ्यूरिक एसिड से 10¹⁶ गुना अधिक मजबूत है। फ्लोरोएंटिमोनिक एसिड का रासायनिक सूत्र HSbF₆ है।

questions.question: The Indian Coast Guard Day celebrated on which of the following date? > भारतीय तट रक्षक दिवस निम्नलिखित में से किस तारीख को मनाया जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Indian Coast Guard day is celebrated on 1st February 2025. This is 49th Raising Day of this indomitable Indian maritime force. It was established on 01 Feb 1977 as a small force. Now it is a formidable maritime security service with over 150 ships and 75 aircraft. The ICG's key achievements include life-saving missions, anti-smuggling efforts, disaster response, and environmental protection. > भारतीय तट रक्षक दिवस 1 फरवरी 2025 को मनाया जाएगा। यह इस अदम्य भारतीय समुद्री बल का 49वां स्थापना दिवस है। इसे 01 फरवरी 1977 को एक छोटे बल के रूप में स्थापित किया गया था। अब यह 150 से अधिक जहाजों और 75 विमानों के साथ एक मजबूत समुद्री सुरक्षा सेवा है। ICG की प्रमुख उपलब्धियों में जीवन-रक्षक मिशन, तस्करी विरोधी प्रयास, आपदा प्रतिक्रिया और पर्यावरण संरक्षण शामिल हैं।

questions.question: Which peak among the following is the highest in altitude in India?
questions.explanation: According to the question Nanda Devi (7816m) is highest peak in India but Kanchenjanga (8586m) is the highest peak of India. Top 5 highest peaks in India in their descending height are K2, Kanchenjunga, Nanda Devi, Kamet, & Santaro Kangri.

questions.question: Leukaemia is a type of cancer that primarily affects which cells in the body? >ल्यूकेमिया एक प्रकार का कैंसर है जो मुख्य रूप से शरीर की किन कोशिकाओं को प्रभावित करता है?
questions.explanation: Leukaema is a cancer of blood which affects WBC and hinders the body’s ability to fight infection. > ल्यूकेमिया रक्त का कैंसर है जो सफेद रक्त कोशिकाओं को प्रभावित करता है और शरीर की संक्रमण से लड़ने की क्षमता को बाधित करता है।

questions.question: Pipe Q can fill the tank in 60 hours and pipe R may fill in 75 hours. Q and R pipes are opened together for 10 hours after which pipe W is also opened simultaneously for 15 hours to reach the half level mark. How much time [in hours] will pipe W, alone take to empty the entire tank?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6a02d9fbf36758.15363049.png) The work done by Q and R in 10 hours = 9×10=90 unit To reach the half level mark they have to fill 150-90 = 60 unit more And also they open pipe W [which is an outlet pipe] for 15 hours, so 15[5+4-W] = 60 W = 5 unit/h So, W will take ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6a02d9fc1e9dc4.47448669.gif) hours to empty the whole tank.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error is in segment [B] "I cannot read or". • The sentence is in the past tense ["When I was young"], so the following verb should also be in the past tense. • "Cannot" [present tense] should be replaced with "could not" [past tense] to maintain correct tense consistency. Correct Sentence: "When I was young, I could not read or write English."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. He tried to _________ the damage caused by his words, but it was too late.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Compensate." Explanation: The verb "compensate" means to make up for a loss, damage, or harm, which fits the context of the sentence. The phrase "compensate for the damage caused by his words" expresses the idea of trying to make up for the harm caused, which makes sense in this situation. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Recover – सुधारना – To regain or return to a normal state after a loss or injury. Example: "After the accident, it took him months to recover fully." • Deny – अस्वीकार करना – To refuse to admit the truth or existence of something. Example: "She denied breaking the vase, despite the evidence." • Compensate – क्षतिपूर्ति करना – To make up for something that was lost or damaged, especially in the case of harm or wrongdoing. Example: "He tried to compensate for his mistake by apologizing." • Delay – विलंब करना – To postpone something or make it happen later than planned. Example: "The flight was delayed due to bad weather."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Were he an actor, everyone will appreciate his acting.
options: Were he an actor, everyone would appreciate his acting.
Was he an actor, everyone will appreciate his acting.
If he were an actor, everyone will appreciate his acting.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: [A] Were he an actor, everyone would appreciate his acting. Explanation: • The sentence uses "Were he", which is a form of the subjunctive mood, used for hypothetical or unreal conditions. • In such cases, the correct verb in the main clause should be "would" instead of "will" because "will" expresses certainty, whereas "would" expresses a hypothetical situation. Corrected Sentence: "Were he an actor, everyone would appreciate his acting."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: A red and a blue car was parked outside the showroom.
A red and a blue car were parked outside the showroom.
A red and blue car were parked outside the showroom.
A red and blue car are parked outside the showroom.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: [B] A red and a blue car were parked outside the showroom. Explanation: • The phrase "A red and a blue car" refers to two separate cars because the article "a" is used before both adjectives [red and blue]. • Since the subject is plural, the correct verb should be "were" instead of "was" or "are".

questions.question: Identify the word that is incorrectly spelt.
questions.explanation: The incorrect spelling is "Battalian." Battalion [Correct] Here are the correct spellings and meanings of all options: • Barrel – बैरल – A large, cylindrical container. Example: "The wine was stored in oak barrels." • Battalion – बटालियन – A military unit. Example: "The battalion marched to the training grounds." • Barren – बंजर – Describing land that is empty or unable to produce crops. Example: "The desert was barren with no signs of life." • Binocular – दूरबीन – A device for magnifying distant objects using two lenses. Example: "He used binoculars to watch the birds in the distance."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. One of my jobs was to hand back the prizes.
questions.explanation: Use "Hand out." • The phrase "hand back" means to return something to its owner, which does not fit the context of distributing prizes. • The sentence suggests that the person was responsible for distributing prizes, which aligns with the meaning of "hand out." Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Hand over – किसी को सौंपना - To give something to someone, often in an official capacity. Example: "The officer asked him to hand over his license." • Hand down – पीढ़ी दर पीढ़ी देना - To pass something from one generation to another or to announce a decision. Example: "The traditions were handed down for centuries." • Hand out – वितरित करना - To distribute something to people. Example: "The teacher handed out worksheets to the students." • Hand back – वापस देना - To return something. Example: "He handed back the book after reading it."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - Friendship is not only the condiment but also the ANODYNE of life.
questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Answer: [B] Painkiller Explanation: The word "anodyne" refers to something that relieves pain or discomfort, whether physically or emotionally. In the context of the sentence "Friendship is not only the condiment but also the anodyne of life," friendship is being compared to an "anodyne" because it provides relief, comfort, or solace in times of emotional pain or hardship. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Anodyne – दर्दनिवारक – Something that relieves pain, either physical or emotional. Example: "Her kind words were an anodyne to his sadness." • Comfort – आराम – A state of physical or emotional ease and freedom from pain or distress. Example: "She found comfort in talking to her best friend after a rough day." • Painkiller – दर्दनिवारक – A substance or thing that relieves pain, usually used in a medical context. Example: "The doctor prescribed a painkiller to ease the patient's discomfort." • Catalyst – उत्प्रेरक– A person or thing that causes or speeds up a change or event. Example: "The new policy acted as a catalyst for economic growth." • Burden – भार – A heavy load, either physically or metaphorically, often causing stress or difficulty. Example: "The financial burden of the crisis affected many families."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. GOODWILL
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Hostility – शत्रुता / विरोध – A feeling of strong dislike, opposition, or unfriendliness. Example: "There was clear hostility between the two rival teams." • Despair – निराशा / हताशा – A complete loss of hope. Example: "After losing his job, he fell into deep despair." • Amity – मैत्री / सद्भाव – A peaceful and friendly relationship. Example: "The two nations signed a treaty of amity to strengthen their relations." • Betrayal – विश्वासघात / धोखा – The act of being disloyal or breaking trust. Example: "She felt deep pain after his betrayal of their friendship." Conclusion: "Hostility" is the most appropriate antonym for "Goodwill," as goodwill refers to kindness and a positive attitude, whereas hostility means unfriendliness and opposition.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. He aspires__________ a scientific career.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition to use in this context is "To." "Aspire" is always followed by "to" when referring to a goal or ambition. Explanation: • On – "On" is used to indicate position or attachment. Example: "The book is on the table."• For – "For" is used to indicate purpose or benefit, but it is not used with "aspire." Example: "This medal is for his achievement." • To – "To" is used with "aspire" when referring to ambitions or goals. Example: "She aspires to a leadership role." • With – "With" is used to indicate association or accompaniment. Example: "He went to the party with his friends."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Jumped the gun
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Option [D] - Acted hastily "Jumped the gun" – "जल्दबाज़ी में काम करना" This idiom means to act too soon or before the right time, often leading to mistakes. It originates from races, where a runner starts before the official signal (gunshot), leading to disqualification. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase comes from track and field races, where athletes who start running before the starter pistol fires are said to have "jumped the gun." It metaphorically applies to acting too early without proper preparation or timing. Example: "He jumped the gun by announcing the product launch before finalizing the details." Hindi: "उसने बिना सभी विवरण तय किए ही उत्पाद लॉन्च की घोषणा कर दी, यह उसकी जल्दबाजी थी।"

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error is in segment [A] "It was him". CONCEPT: • When a sentence starts with "It" followed by any form of "be" [was, is, will be, etc.], the pronoun after "be" must be in the nominative case [subject form]. • "Him" is in the objective case, but it should be "he" [nominative case]. Correct Sentence: "It was he who helped me complete the assignment on time."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. PREJUDICE
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all words along with their usage: Prejudice – पूर्वाग्रह / पक्षपात – A preconceived opinion or judgment about someone or something, often without proper knowledge or understanding. Example: "His prejudice against the new policy prevented him from considering its benefits." Now, let's analyze the given options: • Genius – प्रतिभा / विलक्षण बुद्धिमत्ता – Exceptional intellectual or creative ability. Example: "Einstein was a genius in the field of physics." • Bias – पक्षपात / झुकाव – A tendency to favor one side or viewpoint unfairly, similar to prejudice. Example: "The judge was removed from the case due to his bias towards the defendant." • Victim – पीड़ित / शिकार – A person who suffers harm due to crime, accident, or injustice. Example: "He was a victim of identity theft." • Culprit – दोषी / अपराधी – A person responsible for a crime or wrongdoing. Example: "The police caught the culprit behind the robbery." Conclusion: "Bias" is the most appropriate synonym for "Prejudice," as both words indicate an unfair inclination or opinion.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- Emotion is the INNATE weakness of human.
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all words along with its usage: • Innate – जन्मजात / प्राकृतिक – Existing naturally from birth; not learned or acquired. Example: "Her ability to sing beautifully is innate." Now, let's analyse the given options: • Immense – बहुत बड़ा / विशाल – Extremely large in size or degree. Example: "The immense mountains stretched as far as the eye could see." • Tiny – बहुत छोटा / सूक्ष्म – Extremely small in size. Example: "The tiny bird built its nest in the tree." • Strange – अजीब / अपरिचित – Unusual or unfamiliar. Example: "She heard a strange noise coming from the attic." • Acquired – अर्जित / सीखा हुआ – Gained or developed over time, not present from birth. Example: "Language is an acquired skill that children learn as they grow." Conclusion: "Acquired" is the most appropriate antonym for "Innate," as "Innate" means inborn, while "Acquired" means learned or developed over time.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The morning _______ by the lake had a golden _______ on the water.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "The morning scene by the lake had a golden sheen on the water." This means that the scene [a view or setting] by the lake had a sheen [a soft shine] reflecting on the water. Explanation of the Other Options: • Scene – "दृश्य / दृश्य स्थल" – A view, location, or setting. Example: "The beautiful scene from the hilltop took my breath away." • Sheen – "चमक / आभा" – A soft shine or glow. Example: "Her hair had a golden sheen under the sunlight." • Seen – "देखा गया / देखा हुआ" – The past participle of "see," meaning to have perceived something. Example: "I have never seen such a bright star before."

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A person who writes and edits dictionaries
questions.explanation: Here are the explanations for all options: • Cartographer – "मानचित्रकार" – A person who creates or designs maps. Example: "The cartographer spent months making an accurate map of the new city." • Lexicographer – "शब्दकोश लेखक" – A person who writes and edits dictionaries. Example: "The lexicographer worked for years compiling new words for the dictionary." • Calligrapher – " सुलेखक" – A person skilled in decorative handwriting or calligraphy. Example: "The wedding invitations were written by a talented calligrapher." • Stenographer – "आशुलिपिक / लिपिक" – A person who transcribes speech into shorthand. Example: "The stenographer recorded the court proceedings in shorthand." Conclusion: Lexicographer is the most appropriate choice as it refers to a person who writes and edits dictionaries.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. I often have to make decisions that go against the grain.
options: Oppose or resist natural tendencies or norms
To make something smooth and polished
questions.explanation: Go Against the Grain – स्वाभाविक प्रवृत्ति या परंपरा का विरोध करना "Go against the grain" means to act in a way that is unusual, unnatural, or contrary to what is generally accepted or expected. It refers to resisting norms, traditions, or natural tendencies. Origin of this Idiom: This phrase originates from woodworking, where cutting against the grain of the wood makes it harder to work with, requiring more effort. Over time, it became a metaphor for doing something that feels unnatural or goes against accepted norms. Example: "As an honest journalist, writing a false story would go against the grain for me." [This means that writing a false story would feel unnatural and uncomfortable for the journalist because it contradicts their values.] Hindi Translation: "एक ईमानदार पत्रकार के रूप में, झूठी खबर लिखना मेरी स्वाभाविक प्रवृत्ति के विरुद्ध होगा।"

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. One who runs away from justice or the law
questions.explanation: Here are the meanings and examples of the given options: • Fugitive – "भगोड़ा / फरार व्यक्ति" – A person who runs away from justice, the law, or authorities to avoid punishment or arrest. Example: "The fugitive escaped from prison and has been on the run for years." • Spy – "जासूस" – A person who secretly gathers information about an organization, country, or enemy. Example: "The spy infiltrated the enemy's headquarters to gather intelligence." • Convict – "अपराधी “– A person who has been found guilty of a crime and sentenced by a court. Example: "The convict was sentenced to ten years in prison for robbery." • Prisoner – "कैदी" – A person who is legally confined in prison due to committing a crime. Example: "The prisoner spent years behind bars for his involvement in the crime."

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. This allows them to make informed decisions about their health. B. Wearable technology has become increasingly popular in recent years. C. These devices track various health metrics, such as heart rate and sleep patterns. D. As a result, users can gain valuable insights into their overall well-being.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: BCAD. This order makes sense because: • Sentence B introduces the general topic: the popularity of wearable technology. • Sentence C describes the function of these devices: tracking health metrics. • Sentence D explains the benefit of this tracking: valuable insights into well-being. • Sentence A connects the insights to their practical application: informed health decisions. Final paragraph: " Wearable technology has become increasingly popular in recent years. These devices track various health metrics, such as heart rate and sleep patterns. This allows them to make informed decisions about their health. As a result, users can gain valuable insights into their overall well-being."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The chef is preparing the meal for the guests.
options: The meal is prepared by the chef for the guests.
The meal is being prepared by the chef for the guests.
The meal was prepared by the chef for the guests.
The meal will be prepared by the chef for the guests.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The meal is being prepared by the chef for the guests. Explanation: • Active voice structure: The chef [Subject] + is preparing [Verb] + the meal [Object] + for the guests. • Passive voice structure: The meal [Object] + is being prepared [Verb3] + by the chef [by + Subject] + for the guests. This option correctly changes the voice of the sentence from active to passive, emphasising the object [the meal] rather than the subject [the chef]. The verb tense remains consistent in the present continuous tense with "is being prepared."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The teacher said, “Rajiv, come to my desk with your exercise book now.”
options: The teacher ordered Rajiv to come to her desk with his exercise book now.
The teacher ordered Rajiv to go to her desk with his exercise book now.
The teacher ordered Rajiv to come to her desk with his exercise book then.
The teacher said to Rajiv to come to her desk with his exercise book then.
questions.explanation: Explanation: In indirect speech, imperative sentences [commands or requests] are reported using verbs like "ordered," "requested," or "asked," and time expressions may change accordingly. Changes Applied: • Reporting Verb: "Said" changes to "ordered" since it is a command. • Pronoun Changes: • "My" [teacher’s] → "Her" • "Your" [Rajiv’s] → "His" • Time Expression Change: • "Now" → "Then" The correct answer is: [C] The teacher ordered Rajiv to come to her desk with his exercise book then.

questions.question: One day the old emperor Shahjahan became ill. His son Aurangzeb, who always wanted to be the emperor, put his father in a jail. Jahanara Begum, the eldest child of Shahjahan, did not ____1____ her father and went to jail along with him. She said, “I shall ____2____ the sufferings of my father.
